title: Earthy Juicy Vietnamese Pork Spring-Rolls
description: A midnight-craving Vietnamese dish featuring earthy and juicy pork, wrapped in a thin dough made from all-purpose flour. Perfect for a halal-friendly snack or meal.

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: halal alternative to traditional fish sauce

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and salt. Gradually add in the water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, combine the ground pork, sliced mushrooms, soy sauce, fish sauce, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper. Mix well until all the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces. Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place 1-2 tablespoons of the pork filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the spring roll.
Heat a large skillet or wok with about 1-2 inches of oil over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, add a few of the spring rolls to the skillet (do not overcrowd).
Cook the spring rolls for 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the spring rolls from the oil and drain on paper towels.
Serve the spring rolls hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
